section: Observed route-capacity anchors
evidence_class: observed route capacity
source_basis: IEA/EIA/ADNOC via kmz.2; helps UAE crude, not LNG.

section: Observed route-capacity anchors
evidence_class: observed route capacity
source_basis: EIA/Reuters via kmz.2; does not replace southern Iraqi exports.

section: Observed route-capacity anchors
evidence_class: observed route constraint
source_basis: IEA/EIA via kmz.2; Qatari/UAE LNG must move by sea through Hormuz.

section: Route cushion: oil liquids with alternate pipeline or port options
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 EIA baseline; kmz.2 Saudi East-West bypass; 2y7.5 PortWatch tanker validation
method_note: Low assumes fragile recovery plus partial Red Sea bypass; base assumes substantial East-West utilization but remaining Gulf-route loss; high is full Hormuz-exposed Saudi crude baseline.
double_counting_note: Do not add to total oil row; this is one country component.

section: Route cushion: oil liquids with alternate pipeline or port options
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 EIA origin flow; kmz.2 ADCOP/Fujairah bypass; 2y7.5 PortWatch validation
method_note: Low/base reflect ADCOP/Fujairah offset; high is full EIA Hormuz-origin flow. Mixed crude/product source prevents cleaner split.
double_counting_note: Avoid summing with separate refined-product Gulf aggregate.

section: Route cushion: oil liquids with alternate pipeline or port options
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 EIA Saudi product baseline; IEA Gulf refined-product disruption; 2y7.5 PortWatch tanker validation
method_note: Saudi refined products may have some Red Sea optionality but Gulf product routes remain exposed. Base assumes severe partial closure.
double_counting_note: May overlap with broad oil/products row and LPG/naphtha rows.

section: Route cushion: oil liquids with alternate pipeline or port options
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 EIA UAE product proxy; kmz.2 Fujairah/Oman optionality; 2y7.5 PortWatch validation
method_note: UAE has more non-Hormuz optionality than Qatar/Kuwait, so low/base are reduced relative to full exposure.
double_counting_note: Proxy not route-isolated; avoid summing with EIA origin oil row.

section: No-bypass or severe route limit: LNG and Gulf oil liquids
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 EIA LNG figure data; IEA >300 mcm/d LNG loss; kmz.2 no LNG bypass
method_note: Qatar LNG has no practical bypass. Base approximates IEA-reported >300 mcm/d regional loss allocated mostly to Qatar; high equals 1Q25 run-rate.
double_counting_note: Do not add Bcf/d to oil barrel totals.

section: No-bypass or severe route limit: LNG and Gulf oil liquids
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 EIA LNG figure data; IEA >300 mcm/d LNG loss; kmz.2 no LNG bypass
method_note: UAE LNG is smaller but shares the no-bypass constraint. Low/base/high use partial/full export loss logic.
double_counting_note: Do not add Bcf/d to oil barrel totals.

section: No-bypass or severe route limit: LNG and Gulf oil liquids
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 EIA Iraq baseline; kmz.2 Iraq-Turkey constraint; 2y7.5 PortWatch tanker validation
method_note: Southern Iraq is Hormuz-dependent; low approximates fragile recovery, base approximates severe partial closure, high full southern seaborne exposure.
double_counting_note: Northern Ceyhan volumes are not a substitute for southern exports.

section: No-bypass or severe route limit: LNG and Gulf oil liquids
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 EIA origin flow; kmz.2 no meaningful Kuwait bypass; 2y7.5 PortWatch validation
method_note: No meaningful bypass; low/base/high use recovery/severe/full exposure logic. Source is mixed oil basket, not pure crude.
double_counting_note: Avoid summing with total oil row or refined-products aggregate.

section: No-bypass or severe route limit: LNG and Gulf oil liquids
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 EIA Qatar product baseline; IEA LPG/product disruption; 2y7.5 PortWatch validation
method_note: Qatar product exports are Hormuz-dependent; base assumes most normal product flow disrupted in severe partial closure.
double_counting_note: Includes LPG/naphtha, so avoid summing with LPG aggregate.

section: No-bypass or severe route limit: LNG and Gulf oil liquids
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 EIA Qatar baseline; kmz.2 no practical bypass; 2y7.5 PortWatch validation
method_note: No practical liquid export bypass; low/base/high use recovery/severe/full exposure logic.
double_counting_note: Condensate may overlap with petrochemical/feedstock narratives.

section: No-bypass or severe route limit: LNG and Gulf oil liquids
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 EIA/Iran baseline; kmz.2 notes Jask minimal/stopped; 2y7.5 PortWatch validation
method_note: Sanctioned flows and possible dark activity make this low confidence. High assumes full Hormuz-exposed flow disrupted.
double_counting_note: Shadow-fleet/dark AIS can bias public tracker lower.

section: Delayed export availability: fertilizer, sulphur, aluminium
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 QAFCO production proxy; kmz.6 IFASTAT urea share
method_note: Production proxy, not customs exports. No practical Hormuz bypass for seaborne exports.
double_counting_note: Ammonia input overlaps with urea output.

section: Delayed export availability: fertilizer, sulphur, aluminium
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 WITS/UN Comtrade; kmz.6 IFASTAT urea share; 2y7.5 total traffic validation
method_note: Dry-bulk/product-specific observed counts are not cargo-specific; estimates use severe traffic disruption and Hormuz dependence for eastern Gulf shipments.
double_counting_note: Do not add ammonia and urea as independent fertilizer losses without feedstock accounting.

section: Delayed export availability: fertilizer, sulphur, aluminium
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 USGS UAE note; kmz.4 ADNOC/IEA sulphur evidence; kmz.6 sulphur market balance
method_note: UAE may have more port optionality than Qatar, but Shah/Gulf exports remain materially exposed.
double_counting_note: Sulphur is upstream input; do not add to fertilizer output losses.

section: Delayed export availability: fertilizer, sulphur, aluminium
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 QAFCO production proxy; kmz.6 IFASTAT ammonia share
method_note: Production proxy, not pure export. Base assumes severe disruption to exportable surplus.

section: Delayed export availability: fertilizer, sulphur, aluminium
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 WITS/UN Comtrade; kmz.4 IEA sulphur share; kmz.6 sulphur market balance
method_note: Sulphur is one of the best non-obvious Hormuz rows; no cargo-specific observed counts, so estimates are scenario-based.
double_counting_note: Sulphur is upstream of phosphate fertilizer; avoid double-counting with DAP/MAP impacts.

section: Delayed export availability: fertilizer, sulphur, aluminium
evidence_class: modeled disruption; mechanism inferred from route constraints
source_basis: kmz.1 WITS/UN Comtrade; kmz.4 aluminium evidence; kmz.6 global aluminium share
method_note: Bahrain aluminium is a clean Hormuz-dependent industrial flow; disruption is likely delay/availability rather than immediate production loss.
double_counting_note: Do not generalize to all Gulf aluminium until UAE/Qatar/Saudi route shares are reconciled.
